steps.title: Identify the given angle
steps.content: The figure shows transversal k crossing parallel lines m and n. A 135° angle is marked at the intersection of k and n, in the lower-left region (below n, left of k). At any intersection, adjacent angles are supplementary and vertical angles are equal, so the four angles at that intersection are 135°, 45°, 135°, 45°.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'q = 135': The label q is at the upper-left at the intersection of k and m. By corresponding angles (same relative position at a parallel-line crossing), the corresponding angle at n is also upper-left = 45°. So q = 45°, not 135°. ✗
  • Choice B — 'r = 135': The label r is upper-right at the k/m intersection. The corresponding angle at n is upper-right = 45°. So r = 45°, not 135°. ✗
  • Choice C — 's = 135': The label s is upper-right at the k/n intersection; 135° is marked at the lower-left. These are vertical angles (opposite each other across the intersection), so s = 135°. ✓
  • Choice D — 't = 135': The label t refers to the remaining unlabeled angle at the k/n intersection (lower-right), which is supplementary to 135°: 180° − 135° = 45°. So t = 45°, not 135°. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Only s directly labels the 135° angle at the intersection of k and n. That matches choice C — C is correct.
